pattern: A greedy that always emits from whichever letter has more remaining, but avoids a third repeat by yielding to the other letter, keeps both forbidden triples from ever forming.
hints: Always spending the letter you have more of keeps the counts balanced and prevents one letter from piling up.
The only danger is placing a third identical letter in a row; when that would happen, place the other letter instead.

Track remaining a and b counts. Each step pick the letter with the larger remaining count, but if your last two characters are already that letter and the other still remains, emit the other letter instead.

edge_inputs.description: no a, two b stays under the triple limit
twice as many a as b is the tightest packing
only a present forces aa blocks separated

stress.description: random counts within the existence guarantee (neither exceeds 2x+2 of the other)
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    a = rng.randint(0, min(size, 100))
    lo = max(0, (a - 2 + 1) // 2)
    hi = min(100, 2 * a + 2)
    if lo > hi:
        lo = hi
    b = rng.randint(lo, hi)
    return [a, b]
stress.note: bounds keep a and b within the feasibility window where a valid string exists
